name: Peaches - Halved or Sliced
recipeIngredient: An average of 17-1/2 pounds for 7 quarts, or an average of 11 pounds for 9 pints.
A bushel (48 pounds) yields about 16 to 24 quarts, averaging 2-1/2 pounds per quart.
recipeInstructions:
1. Select ripe, mature fruit that is ideal for eating fresh or cooking.
2. To prepare the peaches:
a. Dip in boiling water for 30 to 60 seconds to loosen skins.
b. Quickly dip in cold water and peel off skins.
c. Cut into halves, remove pits, and slice if desired.
d. Keep peeled fruit in ascorbic acid solution to prevent darkening.
3. Prepare syrup or pack peaches:
a. Very light, light, or medium syrup.
b. Water.
c. Apple juice or white grape juice.
4. For hot packing:
- Place drained fruit in jars with syrup, water, or juice and bring to boil.
- Fill jars with hot fruit and cooking liquid, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
- Arrange halves in layers, cut side down.
5. For raw packing:
- Fill jars with raw fruit, cut side down, and add hot water, juice, or syrup, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
6. Adjust lids and process as follows:
- Hot pack:
Pints: Process for 20 minutes at altitudes of 0-1,000 ft; 25 minutes at 1,001-3,000 ft; 30 minutes at 3,001-6,000 ft; 35+ minutes above 6,000 ft.
Quarts: Process for 25 minutes at 0-1,000 ft; 30 minutes at 1,001-3,000 ft; 35 minutes at 3,001-6,000 ft; 40+ minutes above 6,000 ft.
- Raw pack:
Pints: Process for 25 minutes at 0-1,000 ft; 30 minutes at 1,001-3,000 ft; 35 minutes at 3,001-6,000 ft; 40+ minutes above 6,000 ft.
Quarts: Process for 30 minutes at 0-1,000 ft; 35 minutes at 1,001-3,000 ft; 40 minutes at 3,001-6,000 ft; 45+ minutes above 6,000 ft.
